summary: End-to-end validation of the refactored step-up re-auth flow (ReauthDialog + POST /api/auth/reauth) for the 6-digit Security PIN. Backend: 15/15 pytest cases pass against the preview URL (14 in the provided test_reauth_pin_step_up.py + 1 new case I added for DELETE /auth/me/pin rejecting pin-only payloads). Frontend: Walked a freshly-seeded staff user with a configured PIN through the Account > Security > Reset PIN flow; the global ReauthGate interceptor correctly intercepts the 401, pops the new ReauthDialog in PIN mode, digit stripping + 6-char cap work, toggles between PIN and password render and switch modes, and the success toast shows 'PIN confirmed — you have 5 minutes.' followed by the downstream 'Security PIN reset.' toast.
critical_code_review_comments: identity/router.py reauth handler correctly branches on payload.pin vs password, reuses the shared pin_failed_attempts / pin_locked_until counter with /auth/me/pin/verify, and emits a single auth.reauth success audit row with metadata={factor, reason}. ObjectId is excluded via projection {_id:0}.
ReauthRequest.model_validator enforces 'exactly one of password/pin'; tests confirm 422 for both-at-once and neither, and 422 for wrong-shape PINs (non-digit, <6, >6).

DELETE /auth/me/pin now receives the shared ReauthRequest but explicitly rejects pin-only payloads with a 400 and the message 'Password is required to remove a PIN.' — defence-in-depth preserved. Added a regression test for this.

ReauthDialog.jsx: default mode correctly derived from user.pin_configured; data-reauth-mode is exposed on the DialogContent for testability; 423 response auto-falls-back to password mode and re-fetches /auth/me so UI state stays consistent; optional reason input only rendered when requireReason || reason truthy; submit button disabled-state tracks /^\d{6}$/.test(pin) for pin mode and password.length>0 for password mode.
Toast strings match the spec exactly: 'PIN confirmed — you have 5 minutes.' vs 'Identity confirmed — you have 5 minutes.' (driven by response.data.factor from the backend).
Lockout sharing is genuine: 4 wrong PINs via /auth/reauth + 1 wrong via /auth/me/pin/verify trips the 423 — proving the counter lives on the user document, not per-endpoint.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (dialog open in pin mode, digit strip, toggle pin<->password, 6-digit gating of submit, PIN confirmed toast, downstream pin/reset succeeded)

test_credentials: admin@ccms.app / Admin@ComplianceClinic1 ; throwaway staff reauthui_ce4ef8b3@ccms.app / Reauth1@Strong_ce4ef8b3! with PIN 246813 (rotated to 987654 during test)
seed_data_creation: Created one throwaway staff user via POST /api/auth/users and set a PIN via POST /api/auth/me/pin for the UI walkthrough; each pytest function seeds its own user.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests require REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL to be exported before invoking pytest (Secure cookies will not be set on http://localhost). The global ReauthGate in ReauthProvider intercepts 401 with detail='Re-authentication required for this action.' — you can reproduce the flow quickly by (1) configuring a PIN via /auth/me/pin, (2) visiting /account > Security tab, (3) clicking data-testid=pin-reset-btn and submitting the reset dialog. The new ReauthDialog exposes data-reauth-mode=pin|password on its DialogContent for assertions. Reason input (data-testid=reauth-reason-input) is only rendered when requireReason=true or a defaultReason is supplied via useReauth().requestReauth({requireReason:true,defaultReason:'…'}).
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bug observed. All 15 backend cases green, all frontend assertions green.
